Sclerochloa dura is a type of grass, also known as hard beard grass, that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant Sclerochloa dura has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sclerochloa dura include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Sclerochloa dura is used to make teas, infusions, and inflorescence preparations that are used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.
